preamble: The elision of a final [h] (often derived from historical *s) is the final stage of the Tonogenesis process for falling tones. The loss of laryngeal friction stabilizes the distinctive pitch contour on the preceding vowel.

examples.note: Final stage where the laryngeal coda disappears, leaving the complex falling tones.
The departing tone stabilizes as the final [h] is elided.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
